title: SMITDUVM, SUEZ, SIPEnR, and the Banque des Territoires Inaugurate the Largest Renewable Hydrogen Production and Distribution Station in France, Directly Powered by an Energy-from-Waste Facility
content:
After 12 months of construction, a consortium led by SUEZ, SMITDUVM, SIPEnR, and the Banque des Territoires has inaugurated the largest renewable hydrogen production and distribution station in France, located in CrÃ©teil. This innovative unit, the first in the country to be directly powered by an energy-from-waste facility, will produce one ton of renewable hydrogen per day from household waste through water electrolysis, with the potential to double its capacity.

The project, named H2 CrÃ©teil, is a new energy solution for green mobility in the Ile-de-France region. Starting with its commissioning in October 2025, the station will supply hydrogen to Ile-de-France MobilitÃ©s bus line 103 and household waste collection trucks in the Greater Paris area. The facility, which will be accessible to private vehicles 24/7, is strategically located near major road networks, offering a zero-tailpipe-emission alternative for travel within the low-emission zone of Greater Paris.

This project, which aims to produce hydrogen at a price competitive with diesel, is expected to prevent the emission of approximately 1,500 tons of COâ‚‚ equivalent per year. It is a major step in the regionâ€™s energy transition and aligns with Franceâ€™s national strategy to develop decarbonised hydrogen.
author: Analysts Account
